lines.english: Why are you asleep? Wake up, you ignorant fool!

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਜੀਉ ਦੀਆ ਸੁ ਰਿਜਕੁ ਅੰਬਰਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: jin jeeo deea su rijak anbaraavai |
lines.english: The One who gave you life shall also provide you with nourishment.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੈ ਜੀਵਨੁ ਜਗਿ ਸਚੁ ਕਰਿ ਜਾਨਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: tai jeevan jag sach kar jaanaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: You believe that your life in the world is true. ||1||Pause||
In each and every heart, He runs His shop.
Meditate on the Lord, and renounce your egotism and self-conceit.
Within your heart, contemplate the Naam, the Name of the Lord, sometime. ||2||
Evening has set in, and soon there will be darkness on all sides.
Your life has passed away, but you have not arranged your path.
Says Ravi Daas, O ignorant mad-man,
You may have lofty mansions, halls and kitchens.
don't you realize, that this world is the house of death?! ||3||2||
Soohee:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਤਨੁ ਐਸਾ ਜੈਸੇ ਘਾਸ ਕੀ ਟਾਟੀ ॥
lines.translit: eihu tan aisaa jaise ghaas kee ttaattee |
lines.english: This body is like a house of straw.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਲਿ ਗਇਓ ਘਾਸੁ ਰਲਿ ਗਇਓ ਮਾਟੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jal geo ghaas ral geo maattee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: When it is burnt, it mixes with dust. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕ ਘਰੀ ਫੁਨਿ ਰਹਨੁ ਨ ਹੋਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: ek gharee fun rehan na hoee |1|
lines.english: But you cannot stay in them, even for an instant, after death. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਰ ਕੀ ਨਾਰਿ ਉਰਹਿ ਤਨ ਲਾਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: ghar kee naar ureh tan laagee |
lines.english: And the wife of his house, who was so attached to his body and heart,
Even relatives, family and friends begin to say,
"Take his body out, immediately!" ||2||
Says Ravi Daas, the whole world has been plundered,

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਹ ਤਉ ਭੂਤੁ ਭੂਤੁ ਕਰਿ ਭਾਗੀ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: auh tau bhoot bhoot kar bhaagee |3|
lines.english: runs away, crying out, "Ghost! Ghost!" ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਮ ਤਉ ਏਕ ਰਾਮੁ ਕਹਿ ਛੂਟਿਆ ॥੪॥੩॥
lines.translit: ham tau ek raam keh chhoottiaa |4|3|
lines.english: but I have escaped, chanting the Name of the One Lord. ||4||3||
Raag Soohee, The Word Of Shaykh Fareed Jee:

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਸੂਹੀ ਬਾਣੀ ਸੇਖ ਫਰੀਦ ਜੀ ਕੀ ॥
lines.translit: raag soohee baanee sekh fareed jee kee |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਪਿ ਤਪਿ ਲੁਹਿ ਲੁਹਿ ਹਾਥ ਮਰੋਰਉ ॥
lines.translit: tap tap luhi luhi haath marorau |
lines.english: Burning and burning, writhing in pain, I wring my hands.
I have gone insane, seeking my Husband Lord.
Having wasted my youth, now I come to regret and repent. ||1||Pause||
The fault is with me, and not with my Husband Lord. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੈ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਕੀ ਮੈ ਸਾਰ ਨ ਜਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: tai saahib kee mai saar na jaanee |
lines.english: O my Lord and Master, I do not know Your excellence and worth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੈ ਸਹਿ ਮਨ ਮਹਿ ਕੀਆ ਰੋਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: tai seh man meh keea ros |
lines.english: O my Husband Lord, You are angry with me in Your Mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਲੀ ਕੋਇਲ ਤੂ ਕਿਤ ਗੁਨ ਕਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: kaalee koeil too kit gun kaalee |
lines.english: O black bird, what qualities have made you black?

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪਨੇ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮ ਕੇ ਹਉ ਬਿਰਹੈ ਜਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: apane preetam ke hau birahai jaalee |
lines.english: "I have been burnt by separation from my Beloved."
The lonely soul-bride suffers in the pit of the world.
Without her Husband Lord, how can the soul-bride ever find peace?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਹੋਇ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾਲੁ ਤਾ ਪ੍ਰਭੂ ਮਿਲਾਏ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jaa hoe kripaal taa prabhoo milaae |2|
lines.english: When He becomes merciful, then God unites us with Himself. ||2||
In His Mercy, God has united me with the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਫਿਰਿ ਦੇਖਾ ਤਾ ਮੇਰਾ ਅਲਹੁ ਬੇਲੀ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jaa fir dekhaa taa meraa alahu belee |3|
lines.english: And when I look again, then I find God as my Helper. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਕੋ ਸਾਥੀ ਨਾ ਕੋ ਬੇਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: naa ko saathee naa ko belee |
lines.english: She has no companions, and no friends.
The path upon which I must walk is very depressing.
That is where my path lies.
It is sharper than a two-edged sword, and very narrow.
Soohee, Lalit:

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੇੜਾ ਬੰਧਿ ਨ ਸਕਿਓ ਬੰਧਨ ਕੀ ਵੇਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: berraa bandh na sakio bandhan kee velaa |
lines.english: You were not able to make yourself a raft when you should have.
O Shaykh Fareed, think of that path early on. ||4||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਰਿ ਸਰਵਰੁ ਜਬ ਊਛਲੈ ਤਬ ਤਰਣੁ ਦੁਹੇਲਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: bhar saravar jab aoochhalai tab taran duhelaa |1|
lines.english: When the ocean is churning and over-flowing, then it is very difficult to cross over it. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਥੁ ਨ ਲਾਇ ਕਸੁੰਭੜੈ ਜਲਿ ਜਾਸੀ ਢੋਲਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: hath na laae kasunbharrai jal jaasee dtolaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Do not touch the safflower with your hands; its color will fade away, my dear. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕ ਆਪੀਨੑੈ ਪਤਲੀ ਸਹ ਕੇਰੇ ਬੋਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: eik aapeenai patalee seh kere bolaa |
lines.english: First, the bride herself is weak, and then, her Husband Lord's Order is hard to bear.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਧਾ ਥਣੀ ਨ ਆਵਈ ਫਿਰਿ ਹੋਇ ਨ ਮੇਲਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: dudhaa thanee na aavee fir hoe na melaa |2|
lines.english: Milk does not return to the breast; it will not be collected again. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੰਸੁ ਚਲਸੀ ਡੁੰਮਣਾ ਅਹਿ ਤਨੁ ਢੇਰੀ ਥੀਸੀ ॥੩॥੨॥
lines.translit: hans chalasee ddunmanaa eh tan dteree theesee |3|2|
lines.english: the soul departs, sad at heart, and this body returns to dust. ||3||2||
Says Fareed, O my companions, when our Husband Lord calls,
